geographic_scope: Israeli-held Golan Heights plateau and immediate battle approaches along the 1967 ceasefire line
admin_units: northern and central plateau battle axes
theater_description: Main armored and artillery fighting on the occupied Golan plateau from the Syrian surprise attack to the Israeli stabilization/turnaround around 9 October.
geometry_method_note: Subnational plateau-front footprint; excludes most of interior Syria and excludes broad national-level Israeli rear areas. Intended polygon would follow the tank/artillery battle belt around northern, central, and southern Golan approaches.
military_area_note: Counts forces directly committed on the Golan front, not whole Israeli or Syrian armed forces.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator restricted to civilians physically resident within the exposed Golan battle area; dispersed northern-Israel communities are not rolled in because no stable, auditable polygon-wide resident count was found.
split_reason: Opening surprise attack, emergency reserve mobilization, and rapid shift from Syrian breakthrough to Israeli stabilization make a separate exposure window necessary.
quality_note: Military force and death ranges are reconstructable; civilian death evidence is too sparse for a publishable DR point estimate.

geographic_scope: From the Purple Line eastward into Syrian-held Bashan and approaches toward Damascus
theater_description: Israeli advance beyond the original front line, followed by Iraqi and Jordanian reinforcement and changing front geometry east of the plateau.
geometry_method_note: Cross-border expanding front. Any future polygon would need daily or sub-window mapping because the combat zone widened materially after 10 October.
military_area_note: Would need Israeli forward brigades plus Syrian, Iraqi, and Jordanian forces actually committed east of the plateau.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure base changes sharply as the front moves into Syrian villages and rear areas; no clean denominator reconstructed here.
split_reason: Cross-border advance, new belligerent structure, and shifting civilian footprint make this a separate episode.
quality_note: Exposure base shifts too much for a defensible theater-window DR with current evidence.

episode_name: Sinai opening crossing / Operation Badr window
geographic_scope: Suez Canal east-bank bridgeheads and immediate Sinai approach roads opposite the canal sectors
theater_description: Egyptian assault crossing, establishment of bridgeheads on the east bank, and immediate Israeli efforts to contain or dislodge them.
geometry_method_note: Canal-adjacent corridor only; does not use whole Sinai or whole Egypt. Intended polygon would follow the east-bank bridgehead belt and nearby Israeli counterattack routes.
military_area_note: Counts the Israeli Sinai garrison and reinforcing troops plus Egyptian assault and bridgehead forces in the canal theater.
civilian_area_note: The exposed east-bank desert corridor appears extremely sparse in civilians, but no defensible sub-theater civilian count was found; civilian denominator remains non-publishable.
split_reason: Opening crossing and failed Israeli counterattacks are operationally distinct from the later bridgehead stalemate and west-bank countercrossing.
quality_note: Military exposure and death ranges are usable; civilian denominator and civilian death series are too weak for a publishable DR.

episode_name: Sinai bridgehead attrition and 14 October armor battle
geographic_scope: Canal bridgehead belt and nearby central Sinai armored approaches
theater_description: Relative operational pause in shallow bridgeheads followed by the 14 October Egyptian armored advance and Israeli response.
geometry_method_note: Still canal-adjacent, but force density and frontage differ from the opening crossing window.
military_area_note: Would require better day-level force averages than presently assembled.
civilian_area_note: No auditable civilian denominator for the exposed battle strip assembled here.
split_reason: Violence mode and force posture differ materially from both the opening crossing and the later Israeli canal crossing.
quality_note: Needs better phase-specific military losses and stronger civilian footprint reconstruction.

episode_name: Sinai countercrossing, west-bank fighting, and canal-city exposure
geographic_scope: Deversoir crossing area, west bank of the Suez Canal, encirclement zone, and canal-city bombardment/fighting areas
theater_description: Israeli crossing to the west bank, encirclement operations against the Third Army, and urban/canal-city bombardment and fighting.
geometry_method_note: Mixed corridor-plus-urban geometry; exposure base changes materially and includes both maneuver units and populated canal cities.
military_area_note: Would require separate west-bank maneuver-force exposure and city-specific garrison estimates.
civilian_area_note: Port Said and Port Suez signals exist, but a theater-wide civilian denominator would be misleading because city exposure differed sharply from open desert and crossing corridors.
split_reason: Exposure base changes fundamentally once fighting moves to the west bank and urban canal-city zones.
quality_note: Localized civilian signals exist, but no stable theater-wide civilian denominator or front-wide death split has been reconstructed.
